articles.title: Indore News: 922 Crore-Plus Cars Roll On Indore Roads
articles.description: As per data from the Transport Department, the city has 922 luxury cars priced at one crore rupees or more. What stands out is that the number is growing every year, clearly showing a steady rise in demand for high-end vehicles. Automobile experts say that luxury cars were earlier bought mainly for status but buyers now focus more on comfort, advanced safety systems and modern technology.
articles.content:
Indore (Madhya Pradesh): The city is rapidly changing its image on the national map. Along with being known for cleanliness, food and trade, the city is now emerging as a major center for luxury cars in the state.
Cars worth crores of rupees, which w... [1769 chars]

articles.title: Ola trims its realty footprint to recalibrate operations, cut costs
articles.description: The company has given up over 112,000 sq ft at Prestige-RMZ Startech in Koramangala, giving up the entire seventh and eighth floors in Tower B. Global consulting and technology firm Accenture has taken over the floors, sources said.
articles.content:

articles.title: Big Tech Taps Super Bowl Commercials to Put Human Face on A.I.
articles.description: Big technology companies ranging from Open AI and Anthropic to Amazon and Google are betting on Super Bowl commercials to put a human face on AI
articles.content:
If you believe what you see on TV, then the mysterious technological force known as A.I. is nothing without the help of some human hands.

articles.title: UK must work with China on energy tech or get ‘left behind’, says Octopus boss
articles.description: The boss of Britain’s biggest energy supplier has said the UK must work with China or risk getting “left behind” as he said aims to import Chinese wind farm technology could create thousands of jobs.
articles.content:

articles.title: Gasification technology must suit Indian coal characteristics: Dr V K Saraswat
articles.description: padma vibhushan dr v k saraswat, member of niti aayog, on sunday said, “indian coal typically contains ash content ranging from about 25 to 45 percent, whereas coal from many other countries has comparatively lower ash content. therefore, to operate coal gasification projects at lower cost and with high efficiency over a long period, it is extremely important to select gasification technology that is suitable for the specific characteristics of indian coal.” he was delivering the keynote address at a specia
articles.content:
Dr V K Saraswat, Member of NITI Aayog and other dignitaries at the session on coal gasification on Sunday.
